$comment: Cross-question contradiction register for eval/qa/cases.json. WHY: both ancestor corpora's dominant silent-drift failure was pairs of goldens that individually pass verification but cannot both be true (real prior case: one golden's rubric passed only 'perps are whitespace' while a sibling passed only 'perps are live'). This register records periodic corpus-INTERNAL sweeps that compare goldens against each other (no live service calls). HOW TO RE-RUN: (1) extract per-case entity/number signals (SEP/CAP ids, protocol versions, dollar amounts, counts, dates, statuses) from golden.answer + keyFacts + avoid + graderNotes; (2) cluster cases sharing an entity/id, rank clusters by contradiction risk (shared entity + >=2 cases carrying numeric/status claims); (3) additionally scan cross-cutting fact families the entity clusters miss (network constants: base fee/reserve, close time, supply, retention; moving figures: TVL, AUM, SCF analytics; org/founding facts); (4) for each risk cluster extract just the claim-bearing sentences and judge contradictory / tension / consistent; (5) treat cases listed in cases.json overrides.applied as the more-verified side when they conflict with a non-overridden sibling. VERIFIED CONSISTENT clusters are settled — don't re-litigate them next sweep unless a member case changed.

sweeps.method: entity-cluster scan (SEP/CAP ids, protocol versions, SCF rounds, ~150 curated ecosystem entities) + 38 cross-cutting fact-family scans (network constants, moving dollar figures, statuses, founding facts); 102 clusters with >=2 claim-bearing cases had their claim sentences extracted and manually compared; overridden cases (overrides.applied, deep-verified 2026-07-03) treated as the more-verified side

claims.q-asset-rwa-tokenized-freshness: rwa.xyz reported ~$2.84B distributed asset value (2026-06) [1]; SDF announced Stellar "crossed $2 billion" in RWAs on ~April 11, 2026 with >4x growth in 12 months [2]; Messari's Q1-2026 report tracked ~$1.2-1.5B excluding stablecoins [2].
claims.q-defi-rwa-overview: by mid-2026 the tokenized-RWA stack crossed ~$2.8B (Messari Q1 2026)
claims.q-eco-stellar-rwa-stablecoin-volume: Messari 'State of Stellar Q1 2026' (per Lumenloop roundup, week of May 29 2026) puts the tokenized-RWA stack at ~$2.8B (4th among ecosystems)

contradictions.why: The ~$2.8B figure is attributed to two different sources (rwa.xyz in q-asset-rwa-tokenized-freshness vs Messari in the other two), and Messari's Q1-2026 headline is pinned at two different values (~$1.2-1.5B excluding stablecoins vs ~$2.8B tokenized-RWA stack). Under the standard reading of 'tokenized-RWA stack' (excludes stablecoins) both cannot be Messari's tokenized-RWA number. q-eco-stellar-rwa-stablecoin-volume's graderNotes partially reconcile it ('the $1.2-2B vs $2.8B gap is a scope difference — registered funds vs broad RWA incl. stablecoins'), but that reconciliation is not carried by the other two cases, so a candidate correctly quoting Messari-per-one-golden fails the sibling's framing.
contradictions.disposition: resolved by provenance-bearing overrides on 2026-07-03; retained as historical contradiction evidence and rechecked in the 2026-07-09 corpus-health sweep

claims.q-crp-anchors-by-corridor: MoneyGram's own developer docs put the off-ramp at 174 countries (as of 2026-07; marketing rounds to 170+, older SDF material says 180+) [OVERRIDDEN, deep-verified 2026-07-03]
claims.q-hist-remittance-corridors: stellar.org/products-and-tools/moneygram and the 'three-years-with-moneygram' SDF blog confirm cash<->USDC on Stellar across 170+ countries (the live product page now says 180+, ~475K locations) [graderNotes, verified 2026-06-29]

tensions.why: Both agree on the 174/170+ core figures, but they disagree on the provenance and currency of '180+': the overridden case calls 180+ 'older SDF material' while the sibling calls it the CURRENT live product page. The overridden case is the more-verified side (2026-07-03). Graders on either case should accept 170+/174/180+ with any dated sourcing and never hard-gate one of them.

claims.q-hist-franklin-templeton-benji: $580M+ / 'second-largest RWA on Stellar' figure (Apr 30 2026 anniversary) is fresher vendor-press
claims.q-rwa-projects-tokenizing-stellar: Franklin Templeton (BENJI/FOBXX, ~$654M AUM on Stellar) ... confirmed

tensions.why: Two different pinned AUM figures for the same fund ($580M+ vs ~$654M), each from a different dated read of a moving number, with no cross-reference between the cases. Not mutually exclusive (AUM moves), but a grader hard-gating either number would wrongly fail a candidate quoting the sibling's figure.

claims.q-sep-catalog-list: | **SEP-8** | Regulated Assets — per-tx issuer approval server | Final |
claims.q-sep-8-regulated-assets: Status caveat: sep-0008.md preamble says 'Active' (v1.7.4) but the ecosystem README main table lists SEP-8 as 'Final' — the repo is internally inconsistent.

tensions.why: The upstream stellar-protocol repo itself disagrees on SEP-8's status; the catalog case pins 'Final' (README table) without the caveat while the dedicated case documents the inconsistency. A candidate answering 'Active' (per the SEP file preamble) against q-sep-catalog-list would be marked wrong despite citing a primary source. Graders should accept either status when sourced.

claims.q-eco-defi-market-map: Scout /api/analyze?dimension=categories gives coarse buckets (User-Facing App 204, Tooling 109, Infra 104, Protocol/Contract 78) [undated graderNotes]
claims.q-scf-category-funded-ratio: Verified 2026-06-25: ... Protocol/Contract 47/78 ... Re-verified 2026-06-29 (counts drift): Protocol/Contract 44/86≈51%, User-Facing App 103/204≈50%, Tooling 42/108, Infrastructure 34/97
claims.q-scf-funding-by-category: Time-sensitive snapshot (June 29, 2026 live analytics): User-Facing App — ~204 projects, ~103 SCF-funded, ~$9.19M

tensions.why: Scout category-analytics counts drift between probe dates and the three cases pin different snapshots (Protocol/Contract 78 vs 86; Infrastructure 104 vs 106 vs 97); q-eco-defi-market-map's bucket counts are undated and mix snapshots. q-scf-category-funded-ratio already documents the drift and gates only on the ratio-ranking claim — graders on all three should never hard-gate exact bucket counts, only dated-source discipline.

claims.q-eco-lobstr-wallet: the SCF-total disagreement (Scout $232,000 vs Lumenloop $267,463) is unresolvable — official records confirm rounds 2/17/22 but published only ONE amount ($88,000, SCF #22) — never penalize either total, and a candidate saying 'no official cumulative figure exists' is MORE correct than either number [OVERRIDDEN, deep-verified 2026-07-03]
claims.q-eco-wallets-overview: LOBSTR (Ultra Stellar, SCF ~$232K) — 'All grounded on Scout'

tensions.why: The overridden case establishes that no official cumulative LOBSTR SCF total exists and that $232K (Scout) and $267,463 (Lumenloop) are equally unofficial reconstructions, but the two sibling wallet-survey cases cite ~$232K as flatly 'grounded'. Not mutually exclusive (siblings correctly attribute the figure to Scout), but a grader hard-gating $232K on the sibling cases would wrongly fail a candidate quoting Lumenloop's $267,463 or declining to give a total — the disposition the more-verified overridden case says is MORE correct.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: protocol version history + activation dates (P17/P18/P20/P21/P22/P23/P24/P25/P26/P27)
verifiedConsistent.note: Historical 2026-07-03 snapshot only: the P17-P26 mappings were consistent, and P27 was then queued for the 2026-07-08 Mainnet vote. SUPERSEDED by the 2026-07-09 post-vote sweep below: P27/Zipper activated at ledger 63386819 on 2026-07-08T17:00:10Z, so no current grader may use this historical pending wording.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: CAP statuses and protocol assignments (CAP-35/38/46/51/53/58/59/62/63/65/67/71/73/74/75)
verifiedConsistent.note: CAP-35 Final/P17, CAP-38 P18, CAP-46 series P20, CAP-51+53 P21, CAP-58+59 Final/P22, CAP-62/63/65/67 P23 (CAP-67 Final), CAP-73 Implemented/P26, CAP-74+75 Final/P25, CAP-71 Accepted/P27 — status and protocol number identical everywhere they appear.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: SEP statuses across the catalog and per-SEP cases
verifiedConsistent.note: SEP-1/9/10/12/24/31/46/48 Active; SEP-6 Active with only interactive components deprecated in favor of SEP-24 (the only-partial-deprecation nuance is stated identically in all 3 cases that touch it); SEP-30/38/39/41/43/45/55/57/58 Draft — consistent everywhere. SEP-8 moved to tensions (upstream repo itself inconsistent).

verifiedConsistent.cluster: perps/derivatives status — the ancestor corpora's exact failure class
verifiedConsistent.note: All three agree on both halves: perps are EMERGING (Noether testnet, Stellars Finance mainnet-targeted, Zenex pre-release — so NOT whitespace) AND no perp DEX is confirmed mainnet-live; all three exclude Turbolong as leveraged spot. The whitespace-vs-live split that silently drifted in the ancestor corpora is coherently pinned here, including the Scout-status-vs-self-description discrepancy (directory 'Live' vs own 'testnet') documented inside q-defi-perps-whitespace.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: SCF program mechanics (v7, caps, tiers, totals)
verifiedConsistent.note: Historical 2026-07-03 snapshot: Build/Instawards/Public Goods/Audit Bank/Growth Hack and SDF-vs-SCF distinctions were consistent. SUPERSEDED in part by the 2026-07-09 corpus-health sweep: this cluster omitted q-scf-liquidity-award-amount, while q-scf-award-tiers-list repeated its false 'amount undisclosed' claim. Current official rules publish $50K in XLM base plus an optional additional $50K supplemental award, up to $100K total. Funding-v2 also supersedes the old ~$19.97M/~223 analytics snapshot; current grading remains dated/source/count-basis-gated.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: per-project SCF award totals (Scout directory reconstructions)
verifiedConsistent.note: Aquarius $391K (rounds 17/23/27/30), Soroswap $346,750 (rounds 15/17/21), Phoenix ~$394.5K, Reflector $444,840 (rounds 15/20/26/29), Band $100K, Lightecho $93,110, Orally $48K, DIA $38K, Blend $50K, Hana $132K, xBull $56.2K, OrbitCDP $280K, ApiCharge $79K — every repeated figure matches exactly across cases, and the Blend/Script3 team-lineage caveat (project row under-counts team history) is carried consistently by all three overridden SCF-lending cases plus their graderNotes.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: YieldBlox/Blend V2 oracle-manipulation incident (Feb 2026)
verifiedConsistent.note: Date (Feb 21-22 2026, ~00:25 UTC Feb 22), size (~$10M/~$10.2M), freeze (~48M XLM ≈ $7.2-7.5M by Tier-1 validators), vector (USTRY Etherfuse Stablebond pumped ~$1.06→~$106 on illiquid SDEX poisoning Reflector VWAP), and the app-level-not-protocol-level framing are identical across all five cases.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: Soroswap 'first' claims vs first-AMM factcheck
verifiedConsistent.note: Compatible scoping everywhere: Soroswap = first DEX + DEX aggregator on Stellar SOROBAN; explicitly NOT Stellar's first AMM (native AMMs = CAP-38/Protocol 18, 2021); the factcheck case and the what-is cases agree, and StellarX is consistently a trading UI by Ultra Stellar (not an aggregator, no shutdown claim) across 6 cases.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: 2025-2026 RWA/stablecoin launch dates and issuers
verifiedConsistent.note: CRDT=WisdomTree Sep 12 2025, USDY=Ondo Sep 17 2025 (~5.3% APY, non-US), PYUSD=PayPal Sep 18 2025, clustered around Meridian 2025 Rio (Sep 17-18); EURC=Circle 2023-09-26; CCTP live on Stellar 2026-05-19; BENJI=Franklin Templeton since 2021 (first US-registered mutual fund on public blockchain); Figure YLDS live 2026-05-05; DTCC DTC service planned H1 2027 NOT live; Spiko largest / BENJI second-largest RWA — issuer/date/product attributions identical everywhere, with matching cross-issuer must_avoids.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: MoneyGram Ramps core figures
verifiedConsistent.note: 174 (dev docs, exact) with 170+ marketing rounding, SEP-24 + USDC rails, $5-$2,500 off-ramp per tx, launched via Vibrant/LOBSTR 2022 — consistent across all four (the 180+ provenance question is filed under tensions).

verifiedConsistent.cluster: network constants: fees, reserves, close time, supply, monetary history
verifiedConsistent.note: Base fee 100 stroops, 1 XLM = 10,000,000 stroops, base reserve 0.5 XLM, empty account 2 reserves = 1 XLM, min balance (2+subentries)×reserve, trustline 1 reserve, AMM pool-share 2 reserves, ledger close ~3-5s (~5s average; the '~5 s' one-ledger settle claim sits inside the range), 100B XLM generated 2014, ~1% inflation disabled by vote 2019, ~55B one-time SDF burn 2019 — every repeated constant matches; the two fee cases even declare explicit lane ownership to avoid re-teaching.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: RPC/Horizon roles, rename, retention windows
verifiedConsistent.note: Horizon NOT deprecated (legacy/no-new-features); 'Soroban RPC' renamed 'Stellar RPC' Nov 2024; getEvents ~24h default / ~7-day max; HISTORY_RETENTION_WINDOW default 120960 ledgers ≈ 7 days; Horizon default ~1 month (518400); archive/data-lake extends only getLedgers; Hubble/BigQuery for point-in-time history — the corpus notes this phrasing was deliberately normalized across tooling-infra and it holds in every case checked.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: oracles: Reflector leading but not sole provider
verifiedConsistent.note: All six frame Reflector as the dominant/most-integrated Stellar oracle while explicitly forbidding 'only oracle' claims; Band, RedStone (mainnet since Mar 2026), DIA, Lightecho, Orally named consistently.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: org/founding facts (SDF, SCP, consensus, Tier 1)
verifiedConsistent.note: SDF founded 2014 by Jed McCaleb + Joyce Kim; SCP authored by David Mazières, 2015 rewrite replacing Ripple-derived consensus after a fork; CEO Denelle Dixon; Delaware non-stock nonprofit (not 501(c)(3), pays US taxes); not PoS, no staking; Tier 1 = 3 geographically dispersed Full Validators + earned via quorum inclusion, no static canonical list, SDF quorum ~7 orgs — no conflicts.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: moving TVL figures (chain-wide + Aquarius + Blend)
verifiedConsistent.note: All gate on dated-source + freshness-caveat discipline rather than frozen numbers; the pinned reads agree where they overlap (~$242M chain record May 30 2026, ~$137-140M late-June reads, Aquarius ~$48-52M dossier-era ≈ largest single AMM ~20%); DeFindex TVL honestly declared not-in-corpus.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: wallets and Ultra Stellar product family
verifiedConsistent.note: Freighter = SDF official; LOBSTR/StellarX/StellarTerm = Ultra Stellar (independent, not SDF); Hana multi-chain; xBull = Creit-Tech; StellarX no-shutdown-claim guard consistent in both cases carrying it; passkey-kit = legacy precursor to OpenZeppelin Smart Accounts with smart-account-kit as current default in both smart-wallet cases. (LOBSTR SCF-total nuance filed under tensions.)

verifiedConsistent.cluster: Friendbot/testnet funding boundaries
verifiedConsistent.note: Friendbot testnet/futurenet-only, mainnet XLM must be acquired, testnet periodically resets (~2-4x/yr, futurenet irregular), Lab can add testnet USDC/EURC trustlines but not mint units, network passphrases distinct — consistent across all eight.

sweeps.method: date-contingent P27 trigger re-verification after the scheduled Mainnet vote: sibling scan over every compiled case mentioning Protocol 27, P27, Zipper, or 2026-07-08; live Horizon boundary-ledger probe; stellar-core/CAP source check; SDF upgrade-guide check; independent re-derivation by a second-vendor Solo agent

claims.live network: Horizon/RPC and independent protocol history report Protocol 27; first P27 ledger 63386819 closed 2026-07-08T17:00:10Z
claims.official docs: Software Versions still labels P27 as Testnet-only and shows TBD for the P27 Rust SDK/CLI after stable releases shipped

tensions.why: The live chain and release feeds have advanced while the official version table lags. Current-network and latest-release questions must use Horizon/RPC and GitHub releases; the docs lag is tracked as sd-008.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: Protocol 27 activation, CAP-0071 semantics, and v27 tooling
verifiedConsistent.note: Post-override truth is consistent: P27/Zipper activated on Mainnet at ledger 63386819 on 2026-07-08T17:00:10Z; CAP-0071 provides authentication delegation/address-bound credentials; stellar-core, soroban-sdk, and stellar-cli have stable v27 releases. The edge latest-version case remains behavior-gated rather than number-gated, and the stellar-core identity case never carried an activation claim.

sweeps.method: post-P27 full corpus-health refresh: free internal contradiction scan over all 469 compiled cases using entity/number/status/date signals and eight cross-cutting overlays; 127 shared claim clusters manually adjudicated; mandatory reopen set probed against current free primary/source/live surfaces; risk-weighted 12-case refute batches expanded only when a contradiction cluster was found

claims.official rules: up to $100,000 in XLM total: $50,000 Base Award plus an optional additional $50,000 Supplemental Award

contradictions.why: Published official rules now disclose the complete structure; the prior no-amount answer and trap were false. Soroswap's individual $50,000 award remains compatible with the program maximum.
contradictions.disposition: resolved with provenance-bearing overrides; inherited review text explicitly invalidated

contradictions.why: Two siblings froze the wrong current deadline.

claims.official docs/source: 15 hosted-provider rows; Liquify appears only under the separate publicly accessible APIs section

contradictions.why: The answer conflated two provider categories.
contradictions.disposition: resolved by preserving the hosted/public distinction

claims.brand/issuer: inherited grading treated PayPal as PYUSD's legal issuer
claims.entity time slices: SDF's 2025 launch release names Paxos Trust Company, LLC; Paxos's current product page names Paxos Trust Company, N.A.

contradictions.why: Product brand was conflated with issuer, then one historical Paxos entity was frozen as timeless despite current primary-source drift.
contradictions.disposition: resolved as PayPal-branded/Paxos-issued with explicit dated LLC-versus-N.A. disagreement

claims.inherited avoid: add_identity is an invented API name
claims.current draft: SEP-0057's claim-based reference appendix explicitly contains add_identity

contradictions.why: A moving draft invalidated the false-avoid example.
contradictions.disposition: resolved: current function acknowledged while draft/non-stable status remains explicit

claims.inherited answer: EURC legal issuer is Circle Mint Europe

contradictions.disposition: resolved with current Circle primary disclosure

claims.inherited claims: undefined RWA market superlatives/negative comparisons, an unsupported exact deployment date, reconstructed wallet SCF totals, and an undefined MetaMask-scale comparison
claims.verified core: the named products/projects/operators remain supported, but those comparisons, date, and reconstructed totals are not independently established

contradictions.why: Useful core facts were bundled with unnecessary claims that lacked a defined source methodology.
contradictions.disposition: resolved by removing the unverifiable clauses and retaining the supported core

claims.real-world provider surface: Alchemy's Stellar Data API is live for indexed Stellar data as of 2026-07-08, following Stellar RPC support on 2026-04-16
claims.official Stellar docs surface: the indexers page still says targeted launch H1 2026; stellar/stellar-docs#2573 is open

tensions.why: Provider truth has advanced while the official discovery page remains stale.
tensions.disposition: encoded as an explicit real-world-versus-docs-surface disagreement; never mark Alchemy not live solely from the stale page

claims.Scout corpus: provides reconstructed totals, live roster snapshots, category calculations, and directory candidates
claims.world truth: matching independent official aggregates or provider-by-provider verification are absent

tensions.why: The aggregator is a valid corpus source but cannot independently corroborate its own derived values or discovery roster.
tensions.disposition: corpus-relative encoding: require source, as-of date, method/basis, and external verification before world-truth claims

claims.moving/scoped values: LOBSTR reconstructed SCF totals disagree; MoneyGram uses 170+/174/180+ across dated surfaces; SEP-8 is Active in its file but Final in the repository table

tensions.why: These are disclosed provenance/scope disagreements, not facts that can be collapsed into one timeless scalar.
tensions.disposition: retain grader caution and accept accurately dated/source-scoped answers

mandatoryReopen.fixedUpstreamRootOverrides: reopened builder region/count basis, Orbit lifecycle/canonical slug, Blend funding history, LOBSTR basis, SCF funding-v2 totals/count basis, anchor coverage, and CLI target/source-account cases; fixed roots are labeled without erasing remaining disputes
mandatoryReopen.soroswapAndStorage: Soroswap first/round/program-scope siblings and Protocol-23 storage/TTL auto-restore siblings remain mutually consistent
mandatoryReopen.scfTotalsAndCountBases: funding-v2 reported $40,160,145.13 across 398 projects at the 2026-07-09 probe; category analytics remain source-relative rather than official totals
mandatoryReopen.builderAndAnchorCoverage: live probe returned 19 Latin America builders and 7 payments builders; anchor probe returned 19 total rows and 10 with coverage at probe time, so counts remain dated
mandatoryReopen.cliTargetAndSourceAccount: stellar-cli v27.0.0 and current source confirm wasm32v1-none plus canonical --source-account with --source alias; no stale wasm32-unknown-unknown or global-flag grading remains

riskWeightedRefuteBatches: B NOT CLEAN — deadline, provider classification, deployment-date, and RWA comparison repairs
D NOT CLEAN — stablecoin issuer, wallet totals/comparison, and category aggregate repairs
E NOT CLEAN — SEP-0057 add_identity false avoid
F NOT CLEAN — EURC entity plus Scout-only Phoenix/RFP/category/oracle claims
G NOT CLEAN — PYUSD entity drift plus undefined RWA negative comparison
H CLEAN after all G repairs
I CLEAN — second consecutive clean batch; stop condition satisfied

verifiedConsistent.cluster: Protocol 27 activation and tooling
verifiedConsistent.note: P27/Zipper is active from ledger 63386819 at 2026-07-08T17:00:10Z; the earlier pending note is historical and explicitly superseded.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: SCF award mechanics and Liquidity Award scope
verifiedConsistent.note: Program maximum ($100K in XLM via $50K Base plus optional $50K Supplemental), individual Soroswap award, general Build cap, and current-round deadline are explicitly distinguished.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: Alchemy indexed-data launch versus stale Stellar docs
verifiedConsistent.note: Alchemy Data API live status and the stale H1-2026 Stellar docs statement coexist only as a disclosed surface disagreement; RPC hosted/public provider categories remain separate.

verifiedConsistent.cluster: RWA attribution, stablecoin issuer roles, wallets, SCF source scope, and mandatory reopen families
verifiedConsistent.note: Dated/provenance-bearing overrides now distinguish source snapshots, legal entities, directory reconstructions, and current CLI/API semantics without unsupported comparisons.

dateContingentTraps.$comment: Golden traps/claims that are TRUE now but expire on a known future event. After the trigger passes, re-verify each listed case live (golden-truth skill) — an expired trap punishes the then-true claim and becomes a judge-artifact factory. Entries are removed once re-verified; corpus-health sweeps and eval rounds should check this list for passed expiries.
